county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Decatur holds deed records from 1836. Adams County was settled largely by Swiss Mennonite and German families from Pennsylvania and Ohio. Check Mennonite church records in Berne and the Swiss Heritage Village archives for immigration records and family lineages. The Berne Swiss community maintained German-language newspapers (Berne Witness) into the 20th century — valuable for obituaries. BLM-GLO federal land patents document original settlers. Adams County Historical Society maintains local collections.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1835. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1835. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Adams County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Amish and Mennonite Church Records — Adams County
source_profiles.repository: Mennonite Historical Library, Goshen College / Heritage Historical Library, Aylmer ON / Local church records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Amish church district records, family group sheets, Budget and Die Botschaft newspaper correspondence, and Mennonite church records. Records may be in German/Pennsylvania Dutch. Mennonite Historical Library at Goshen College is a premier repository. Family histories compiled by Amish genealogists are often in private collections.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Amish records in private family collections; Mennonite Historical Library has compiled indexes and microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Fort Wayne. Allen County is home to the Allen County Public Library (ACPL), which has the second-largest genealogy collection in the United States after the Family History Library in Salt Lake City. The ACPL Genealogy Center holds over 350,000 printed volumes, 600,000+ microforms, and extensive databases. Fort Wayne was a major trading post at the confluence of three rivers (St. Marys, St. Joseph, Maumee). The Three Rivers area was Miami Indian territory — check NARA for Miami removal records (1840s). Strong German Lutheran and Catholic church records essential.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1823. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1823. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.repository: Allen County Clerk (Circuit/Superior Court), Fort Wayne
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1823. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1823. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Allen County Auditor, Fort Wayne / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1823. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Allen County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wabash and Erie Canal (longest canal ever built in North America, 468 miles) and Whitewater Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. Canal workers included many Irish immigrants. Canal land grants at Indiana State Archives.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Indiana State Archives; some published by Indiana Historical Bureau

source_profiles.repository: Allen County Clerk, Fort Wayne / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana provided approximately 197,000 soldiers to the Union Army. Adjutant General records, enlistment papers, and discharge records at Indiana State Archives. Indiana Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Indianapolis). W.H.H. Terrell's 'Report of the Adjutant General of the State of Indiana' (8 vols.) published rosters.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Columbus. Columbus is internationally renowned for modern architecture (Eero Saarinen, I.M. Pei) but its genealogical value lies in Bartholomew County Historical Society collections and well-preserved county records from 1821. Cummins Engine Company (1919) employment records document 20th-century workers. Check Indiana State Library for microfilmed county records. Federal land patents at BLM-GLO. Early settlers came from Kentucky and the Carolinas via the Ohio River and overland routes. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records from early settlement.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Fowler. Created 1840 from Jasper County. One of Indiana's flattest and most agricultural counties on the Grand Prairie. Late settlement compared to southern Indiana — early records are sparse before 1840. Benton County Historical Society in Fowler maintains family files. BLM-GLO federal land patents document original prairie settlers. Pine Village and Oxford area records. Strong Methodist church records. Benton County was a major grain-producing area — check agricultural census records for family economic data.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1840. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Hartford City. Created 1838 from Jay County. Indiana Gas Belt county — Hartford City boomed in the 1890s when natural gas was discovered. Gas boom attracted glass factories and other industries. Hartford City Glass Company and other factory records document worker families. Blackford County Historical Society. Check Jay County for pre-1838 records. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Small county with good record survival. Indiana natural gas boom newspapers valuable for notices.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1838. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Lebanon. Created 1830 from Hendricks and Marion counties. Boone County Historical Society in Lebanon. Thorntown was an early settlement in the Thorntown Heritage Museum area. Federal land patents at BLM-GLO. Strong Quaker presence — Thorntown and Hazelrigg area Friends meeting records. Lebanon was an important stop on early transportation routes. County Clerk holds marriage records from 1831. Big Eagle Creek and Sugar Creek valley settlers. Check Hendricks and Marion counties for pre-1830 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1830. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Boone County
source_profiles.repository: Earlham College Friends Collection, Richmond / Indiana Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Indiana Yearly Meeting and Western Yearly Meeting records. Quakers kept meticulous records. Indiana was a major destination for Quaker migration from the Carolinas and Virginia — check origin meeting records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed by FamilySearch and held at Earlham College

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Nashville. Created 1836 from Monroe, Jackson, and Bartholomew counties. Brown County is famous as an artists' colony (T.C. Steele, the Hoosier Group) but its rugged hills meant isolated settlements. Brown County Historical Society in Nashville. Many families lived in remote hollows — church records (Baptist, Methodist) are critical supplements to county records. Brown County State Park area. Some of the most isolated communities in Indiana — early tax records serve as census substitutes. Check parent counties for pre-1836 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Delphi. Created 1828 from unorganized territory (originally Cass). Wabash and Erie Canal passed through Carroll County — canal construction records at Indiana State Archives document workers and landowners. Delphi was a canal town. Carroll County Historical Society and Wabash and Erie Canal Association. Deer Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong early Methodist and Presbyterian church records. Check for pre-1828 records in state archives. Flora and Camden area records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Logansport. Created 1828 from Carroll (unorganized territory). Logansport was a major canal junction (Wabash and Erie Canal) and later a railroad center — Pennsylvania Railroad records document workers. Cass County Historical Society. Significant Miami Indian presence — Logansport was near the Forks of the Wabash, a Miami gathering place. Miami removal (1840s) records at NARA. France Park area. Strong German and Irish immigrant records. Check Indiana State Archives for canal-era records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1828. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Washington DC / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Miami Nation records including treaties, removal records (1846), land allotments, annuity rolls, and agent correspondence. Some Miami families received individual land allotments and remained in Indiana. Chief Richardville (Peshewah) and Meshingomesia's band records. Treaty of St. Mary's (1818), Treaty of Paradise Spring (1826).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records at NARA Record Group 75; some at Indiana State Archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wabash and Erie Canal (longest canal ever built in North America, 468 miles) and Whitewater Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Jeffersonville. One of Indiana's original counties (1801), created from Knox County. Clark's Grant (George Rogers Clark's Illinois Regiment land grant, 150,000 acres) — NOT federal land. Check Virginia records and Clark's Grant records at Indiana State Archives. Clarksville, founded 1784, is one of Indiana's oldest settlements. Falls of the Ohio area. Jeffersonville and Clarksville are directly across the river from Louisville, KY — cross-reference Jefferson County, KY records. Howard Steamboat Museum documents river commerce families.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1801. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Clark's Grant Land Records — Clark County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives, Indianapolis / Indiana State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: George Rogers Clark's Illinois Regiment land grant (150,000 acres) in southeastern Indiana. Soldiers who served under Clark in the Revolutionary War received land warrants. This area was NOT part of the federal land system — use Virginia/Illinois records, not BLM-GLO. Early Clarksville (1784) was Indiana's first American settlement.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records at Indiana State Archives and Indiana State Library; some published in Indiana land record compilations

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records — Clark County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Historical Society / Levi Coffin House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana was a free state but had Black Codes restricting African American settlement. Court records include freedom suits, fugitive slave cases, and Black Code enforcement. Levi Coffin of Fountain City (Wayne County) was the 'President of the Underground Railroad.' Quaker meeting records document abolitionist activity.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Indiana State Archives; some published accounts

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio River Commerce Records — Clark County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Local historical societies / Kentucky river communities
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Steamboat records, ferry licenses, river pilot records, and flatboat commerce. Ohio River counties had close economic ties to Kentucky — cross-reference Kentucky county records across the river. Early Indiana settlement came primarily via the Ohio River.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ferry licenses at county level; steamboat records at NARA and historical societies

source_profiles.repository: Clark County Clerk, Jeffersonville / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
